ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_00002473 (tuna salad sandwich) is the closest parent; the Sinpopo product uses a grilled handmade soft bun rather than sliced bread and adds whipped butter, so an exact match is not available.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FKP (tuna sandwich) is the closest available code; the product is a bun-format item with mayo and whipped butter rather than a standard sandwich.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No sufficiently specific SNOMED CT concept exists for a tuna mayo bun product of this type.

serving.natural_language_portion: One tuna mayo bun weighing approximately 150 g, comprising a grilled handmade soft bun filled with seasoned tuna-mayo mixture and spread with whipped butter.

nutrient_source.title: Tuna salad sandwich on white bread — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 21274) adapted with bun format and mayo-butter filling
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry exists for a Sinpopo-style tuna mayo bun in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA Branded Foods. Values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy tuna salad sandwich (NDB 21274) and USDA FoodData Central canned tuna in water (FDC 175159) combined with a soft enriched bun profile. The mayo and butter components increase fat and energy relative to a plain tuna sandwich. Per-100g values reflect a composite estimate: approximately 40% soft bun, 45% tuna-mayo filling, 15% whipped butter spread. Confidence is medium.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; white bread and tuna sandwich analogous estimates
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for a tuna mayo bun. GI estimated at 45 based on the analogous profile of a tuna salad sandwich on white bread (GI ~45, University of Sydney GI Database) adjusted for a soft enriched bun base. The protein and fat content from tuna and mayo attenuate the glycemic response of the bun carbohydrates. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (45 × 37.8) / 100 ≈ 17.0, using 150 g serving with 25.2 g carbohydrate per 100 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (~420 mg per 100 g; ~630 mg per serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit this item to occasional consumption, as one serving contributes approximately 630 mg sodium — about 27% of the 2,300 mg daily limit — primarily from canned tuna brine, mayonnaise, and the seasoned filling.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load (~17 per serving) from refined bun carbohydrates, partially offset by protein and fat
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should consume this bun in moderation; the soft wheat bun raises blood glucose, but the tuna-mayo filling's protein and fat slow gastric emptying, yielding a moderate glycemic load of approximately 17 per serving — portion control and pairing with non-starchy vegetables is advised.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from mayonnaise and whipped butter (~3.2 g per 100 g) and moderate sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: For individuals with cardiovascular disease, the saturated fat from mayonnaise and whipped butter (~4.8 g per serving) and the sodium load (~630 mg per serving) warrant moderation; however, the omega-3 fatty acids from tuna provide a cardioprotective benefit that partially offsets these concerns.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Sinpopo's Tuna Mayo Bun is a Singaporean Western-fusion café item served at Sinpopo Coffee outlets. It consists of chunked canned tuna tossed in seasoned mayonnaise, loaded into a grilled handmade soft enriched bun that is spread with special whipped butter. The bun is soft and pillowy with a lightly toasted exterior from grilling, while the filling is creamy, savoury, and mildly tangy from the mayo seasoning. The dish sits at the intersection of a classic Western tuna salad sandwich and the Singaporean kopitiam-style kaya-butter bun tradition, replacing sweet kaya with savoury tuna mayo. Nutritionally, a 150 g serving provides approximately 372 kcal, 17 g protein, 18 g fat, and 38 g carbohydrate. The sodium content is moderate-to-high at around 630 mg per serving, largely from canned tuna brine and mayonnaise. The glycemic load is moderate (~17 per serving) due to the refined bun, but is attenuated by the protein and fat in the filling. Tuna contributes omega-3 fatty acids (EPA and DHA), vitamin B12, selenium, and niacin. Clinically, this item is suitable for most adults in moderate portions but warrants attention for individuals managing hypertension (sodium), Type 2 Diabetes (refined carbohydrate bun), or cardiovascular disease (saturated fat from mayo and butter). It contains the major allergens gluten, egg (mayonnaise), fish (tuna), and milk (butter).
common_user_queries: What is in Sinpopo's tuna mayo bun?
How many calories in a tuna mayo bun?
Is tuna mayo bun suitable for diabetics?

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional entry for Sinpopo's Tuna Mayo Bun exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA Branded Foods. Values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy tuna salad sandwich and component-level USDA FoodData Central entries for canned tuna, soft enriched bun, and mayonnaise. The composite estimate is internally consistent and plausible but cannot be verified against a direct laboratory analysis or a primary Singapore database entry.
notes: Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted USDA SR Legacy source for this composite food type. GI is an analogous estimate (GI 45) based on the University of Sydney GI Database tuna salad sandwich profile; no direct GI measurement for this specific product exists. Halal status is listed as false because Sinpopo Coffee is not certified halal as of the accessed date; consumers should verify current certification. The serving size of 150 g is an estimate based on typical café bun weights; the actual product weight may vary. Trans fat is reported as 0.1 g per 100 g reflecting trace amounts from dairy-derived butter; this is naturally occurring trans fat (vaccenic acid), not industrial trans fat.
